dataset: Perceived Stress Scale (PSS-10) - Student Well-Being Study

pi_items.verified_value: Perceived Stress Scale (PSS-10) - Student Well-Being Study
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 58: rpc("add_survey", [key, 0, "Perceived Stress Scale (PSS-10) - Student Well-Being Study", "en"])

Official PSS-10 literature confirms items 4, 5, 7, 8 are reverse-scored. Web search results from multiple authoritative sources (CMU Psychology Lab, Mentalyc, Blueprint, etc.) confirm: 'Items 4, 5, 7, and 8 are reverse-scored by subtracting the response from 4.'
Official PSS-10 has 10 items total. Reverse-scored items are 4, 5, 7, 8. Therefore, forward-scored items are 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 10.

Official PSS-10 interpretation cutoff scores from multiple authoritative sources: 'A score between 0–13 is considered low stress' (web search results from mdapp.co, mentalyc.com, blueprint.ai, resref.com)
Official PSS-10 interpretation cutoff scores from authoritative sources: '14–26 is moderate stress'
Official PSS-10 interpretation cutoff scores from authoritative sources: '27–40 is high stress'

privileged_info_summary: The task implements the authentic Perceived Stress Scale (PSS-10), a widely-used psychological assessment measuring perceived stress over the past month on a 0-40 scale. Items 4, 5, 7, and 8 are correctly identified as reverse-scored questions (responses inverted: 0→4, 1→3, 2→2, 3→1, 4→0). Items 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 10 are forward-scored. The assessment rule cutoff scores are standard: low stress (0-13), moderate stress (14-26), and high stress (27-40). The survey is synthetically generated for testing purposes using LimeSurvey's JSON-RPC API with authentic PSS-10 question text.
